name: Cerros da Carrapateira Trail
name_original: Cerros da Carrapateira — percurso circular na Costa Vicentina
description: Jog the moderate 8.2-mile Cerros da Carrapateira Trail, offering diverse terrain and panoramic coastal views in Portugal's natural park.
region_intro: Jogging around Bordeira offers diverse and dramatic landscapes within Portugal's Costa Vicentina Natural Park. The region features expansive beaches like Praia da Bordeira, backed by impressive rolling dunes and rugged limestone cliffs that provide panoramic Atlantic views. Inland, the landscape includes the meandering Ribeira da Bordeira river, which forms a calm lagoon, alongside pleasant wooded areas such as cork oak and pine forests. This varied terrain provides a unique backdrop for running. Best jogging routes around Bordeira The most popular jogging route is Pontal da Carrapateira — circular route in Costa Vicentina, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views along the dramatic cliffs of Pontal da Carrapateira. Another top favourite among local runners is Praias do Amado e Murração — circular route in Costa Vicentina, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This route explores the coastline around Amado and Murração beaches, featuring varied terrain. Local runners also love the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through diverse coastal and inland landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Jogging around Bordeira is defined by expansive beaches, rugged coastal cliffs, and varied wooded areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes with elevation gain. The routes in Bordeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Bordeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bordeira?
A: Bordeira offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging runs with elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Bordeira?
A: Yes, Bordeira has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail, and many paths are accessible and suitable for all skill levels, particularly those along the expansive beaches and boardwalks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running in Bordeira?
A: Jogging in Bordeira offers incredibly diverse and dramatic landscapes. You'll experience expansive golden sand beaches like Praia da Bordeira, backed by impressive rolling dunes and rugged limestone cliffs with panoramic Atlantic views. Inland, routes meander through pleasant wooded areas, including cork oak and pine forests, and alongside the serene Ribeira da Bordeira river and its calm lagoon.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Bordeira?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Bordeira are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420584903/pontal-da-carrapateira-circular-route-in-costa-vicentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal da Carrapateira — circular route in Costa Vicentina&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning coastal views. Another great choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421001212/praias-do-amado-e-murracao-circular-route-in-costa-vicentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praias do Amado e Murração — circular route in Costa Vicentina&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the coastline around these beautiful beaches.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Bordeira?
A: The Costa Vicentina Natural Park, where Bordeira is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails, provided they are kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always check specific trail regulations, especially in protected dune areas or during nesting seasons, but many of the coastal and inland paths are suitable for running with your canine companion.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging options in Bordeira?
A: Absolutely! Bordeira offers several family-friendly jogging options. The boardwalk to Praia da Bordeira is an excellent choice for a gentle run with children, offering spectacular panoramic views over the dunes. Shorter versions of trails like the Bordeira Hiking Trail also provide accessible paths through varied landscapes, suitable for families.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or attractions to look out for on the routes?
A: While jogging in Bordeira, you can discover several captivating natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira da Carrapateira Lagoon and Dunes&lt;/a&gt; offer a serene environment. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/914730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Lake of the Southwest Coast&lt;/a&gt; or even a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7228970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden Lake East of Bordeira&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic cliffs and expansive beaches themselves are major attractions, providing breathtaking vistas.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Bordeira?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads and beaches in Bordeira, such as Praia da Bordeira and Carrapateira village. These areas serve as convenient starting points for many running routes, including loops that explore the coastline and inland areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Bordeira?
A: The best time of year for jogging in Bordeira is typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is pleasantly mild, and the natural vegetation, especially wildflowers in spring, is at its most vibrant. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures but can be windier.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Bordeira?
A: The running routes in Bordeira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners often praise the region's wild, untouched beauty, the dramatic coastal views, and the variety of terrain, from sandy beaches to shaded wooded paths. The sense of seclusion and immersion in nature are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the jogging routes in Bordeira?
A: Yes, particularly around Carrapateira village and near Praia da Bordeira, you can find cafes and restaurants. These spots are perfect for a post-run refreshment or meal, allowing you to enjoy local cuisine and relax after exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any permits required for jogging within the Costa Vicentina Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for jogging on the established trails within the Costa Vicentina Natural Park. However, as it is a protected area, it's important to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife. For more detailed information on the park, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitalgarve.pt/en/10064/from-bordeira-to-the-sea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitalgarve.pt&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Carrapateira, located on the western coast of the Algarve in Portugal, belongs to the parish of Bordeira, in the municipality of Aljezur. Known for Praia do Amado, it is a popular destination for surfers. The locality includes the Carrapateira Fort, the Main Church and the Carrapateira Museum of the Sea and Land. The village is a center of natural beauty, with spacious beaches and dunes, and has remains of an Islamic settlement.

A viewpoint demarcated by a concrete construction. From this point there is a privileged view of the southeast of the Costa Vicentina. The place is quite bucolic and peaceful, making it an ideal stopping point. From here onwards the route begins its return.

Almost at the end of the route you have your first view of the coast, after a few kilometers walking through the interior of Costa Vicentina. Praia da Bordeira appears on the horizon, and the beauty of the contrast with the Atlantic is fascinating.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible from the village of Carrapateira. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it traverses the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentina Coast Natural Park, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect the diverse wildlife and flora, and to respect other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The ideal times to jog the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and allowing you to fully appreciate the blooming wildflowers or the vibrant autumn colors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to access the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail. It is freely accessible to the public, as are most trails within the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentina Coast Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, primarily consisting of dirt paths suitable for trail jogging. You'll also encounter some sections with paved roads and cobblestones, as well as hilly and sandy stretches. Be prepared for rolling landscapes, valleys, ravines, and coastal paths with boardwalks and climbs to cliffs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience stunning panoramic views and notable landmarks. Key highlights include the charming Carrapateira Village, the Viewpoint with Geodetic Marker – Costa Sudoeste, and the impressive View of Praia da Bordeira. You'll also pass through diverse landscapes, from coastal cliffs to inland woods.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail is part of a larger network of routes. It fully encompasses the 'Cerros da Carrapateira' trail and also passes through sections of the 'EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route - part Portugal 4', the 'Historical Way', and the 'Rota Vicentina - Fishermen's Trail'.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while jogging the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various birds like jackdaws, birds of prey, nightingales, and woodpeckers. Mammals such as hare, rabbit, hedgehog, fox, and wild boar also inhabit the area. Along the coast, keep an eye out for otters near Praia da Bordeira.

faqs.question: Is the Cerros da Carrapateira Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, the trail involves some challenging climbs and varied surfaces, including sand and hilly sections. It's not a flat, easy run, so good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended. Beginners with some trail running experience or a good level of general fitness might find it manageable, but it's not ideal for absolute novices.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly notable for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is notable for its exceptional ecological diversity within the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentina Coast Natural Park, offering a unique blend of stunning coastal scenery and serene inland rural beauty. It provides an excellent opportunity for runners to experience a wild and well-preserved part of the Algarve coast with consistently panoramic views.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and moderate difficulty, it's advisable to bring appropriate trail running shoes for good grip. Carry enough water, especially during warmer months, as there are no water sources directly on the trail. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also recommended due to exposed sections, and a light jacket might be useful for coastal breezes.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint with Geodetic Marker – Costa Sudoeste
